attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content:
I had a very intelligent friend analyze this project and create a list of issues that need to be addressed. I want you to skip major issue number one, but I want you to review and come up with a plan to address all the other major and minor issues that are called. I want you to share with me a plan that we can execute step by step, addressing each of the issues. 

Review: Severity-ordered                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                   
  🔴 Major 1 — The submission is not packaged. Reviewer cannot run the system.                                                     
                  
  What: Repo root has no README.md, no requirements.txt, no .env.example, no RATIONALE.md, no transcripts directory, no demo flag
  in run.py. Your own docs/submission_packaging_plan.md is entirely unchecked: every checkbox in Phase A through F is open.

  Why it matters: The assignment is explicit — "Must be executable by reviewers", "Setup instructions (dependencies, API keys)",
  "Sample queries or demo mode", "should indicate whether search was used". And your own plan says: "A failure here invalidates
  everything else."

  Risk if unfixed: Hard fail. Reviewers don't grade code they can't run. Even if they read the prompts and rationale, missing
  Greenhouse fields (run instructions, transcripts, video, written rationale) is a non-completion. This is the only finding here
  that's a true gate — fix this first, regardless of any other feedback.

  Cost: ~2–4 hours to do Phase A + C + D well.

  ---
  🔴 Major 2 — Judge calls do not set temperature=0. Iteration narrative has noise baked in.

  What: judge.py:134-140 calls _client.messages.create() without a temperature parameter. The Anthropic SDK default is 1.0, not 0.
  The comment on line 131 even claims "at temperature=0 the output is deterministic" — but the code never enforces that. The
  iteration log explicitly attributes 4+ score movements during the v4 regression test to "judge variance" — that's the symptom.

  Why it matters: Several of your headline V4.5→V4.6 findings rest on stable judging:
  - multihop-3 CO 2→3 (your own log: "V4.6 did not change the disambiguation trigger from V4.5; this recovery is most likely
  run-to-run variance")
  - noisy-1 / insuff-2 / insuff-4 AQ improvements (also flagged as minor / variance)
  - The "no regression vs V4.5" claim across 18 cases × 6 dims

  You're claiming a 1-clause prompt edit drove these movements, but with a stochastic judge, a meaningful fraction of the delta is
  noise.

  Risk if unfixed: A reviewer who reads judge.py will spot this in seconds. It undercuts the experimental rigor the entire
  submission is designed to showcase. The whole premise — "define behavior → measure it → change it → explain the result" — depends
   on a stable measurement layer.

  Quick fix: Add temperature=0 to the messages.create() call. Re-run the judge on the V4.5 and V4.6 logs (traces are frozen, so
  only judging re-runs). If the V4.6 verdict holds, you have hardened evidence; if it shifts, you've caught a real overstatement
  before the reviewer does.

  ---
  🟠 Major 3 — V4.6 score table is largely saturated. Eval has hit its ceiling.

  What: V4.6 scores 3 on roughly 96 / 108 cells. ES = 3 on all 18 cases. CO = 3 on all 18. CV = 3 on all 18. The only non-3s sit on
   the I-008 trio plus a couple of AQ=2 verbose cases.

  Your own rubric says: "Use the full range. A dimension where every case scores 3 is not discriminating."

  Why it matters: A reviewer reads this as "the eval can't tell the difference between a good prompt and a great one at the top of
  the curve." Three of your six dimensions provide zero signal at V4.6. The argument that V4.6 is "best overall" loses force when
  there's no headroom to be better in.

  Risk if unfixed: The reviewer questions whether the eval can detect a regression you'd actually care about. This is also an
  unforced credibility hit because you can defuse it cheaply in writing — frame the saturation as expected (the eval was designed
  to surface V0/V1 failures, not differentiate V4 from V5), call out I-008 as the one remaining discriminator, and list "harder
  eval set" as a Future Improvement (you already do).

  ---
  🟠 Major 4 — V4.6's margin over V4.5 is small and partially confounded with judge variance.

  What: V4.6 differs from V4.5 by one clause: "This closing sentence is required — do not omit it." The score deltas attributed to
  that change:
  - ambig-1: HO 2→3, TE 2→3 (plausibly the prescriptive signoff working)
  - multihop-3: HO/TE/CO each 2→3 — your own log says this is variance, not the signoff change
  - AQ recoveries on noisy-1, insuff-2, insuff-4 — also flagged minor/variance

  Why it matters: "V4.6 is the final prompt" is the headline of the entire arc. If a reviewer asks "how do you know V4.6 isn't just
   a lucky run of V4.5?", your current answer is one-line prompt change + a few cells of variance.

  Risk if unfixed: Lower than Major 3 alone, but compounds with Major 2 (no temperature=0). Together they read as "the methodology
  is not tight enough to support the final-version claim."

  Cheap mitigation, no re-run needed: In the rationale, explicitly say: "V4.6's contribution is one targeted change — making the
  disambiguation signoff non-optional. The ambig-1 recovery is attributable to that change. Other deltas vs V4.5 are within
  run-to-run noise and not claimed as wins." Honest framing > overclaiming.

  Stronger fix: Add temperature=0 and re-judge V4.5 and V4.6 once. If the V4.6 verdict holds, claim it. If not, the rationale
  becomes "V4.5 = V4.6 within noise; we kept V4.6 because the signoff requirement is conceptually clearer."

  ---
  🟡 Minor 1 — H1a confirmation is mis-attributed.

  Your iteration log V0 section: "H1a: not confirmed here. The model searched before answering in most cases. H1a was confirmed
  later in V1..." Yet project_state.md and (presumably) the rationale narrative say "H1a confirmed: V0/V1 bypass search on familiar
   questions."

  That's directionally misleading. V0 searched fine; V1's conciseness instruction is what broke search. The actual story is more
  interesting — a single answer-format instruction created tool-use fragility — and shows sharper engineering judgment if told
  accurately. Recommend correcting this in the rationale doc; don't carry the V0/V1 conflation into the final write-up.

  ---
  🟡 Minor 2 — TE=2 on noisy-1 / partial-1 / noisy-2 is a borderline judge call.

  The model abstained on evidence_condition: sufficient cases. Rubric TE=2 is "addresses the question but misses a sub-part / picks
   one interpretation". TE=1 is "does not answer the question asked." Pure abstention on a sufficient case is closer to TE=1. The
  judge gave TE=2 across all three. Defensible (it's not wildly off-topic), but a sharp reviewer may push.

  Mitigation: Pre-empt it in failure analysis — explicitly call out that you treat retrieval-ceiling abstention as TE=2 rather than
   TE=1 and explain why (the model correctly applied evidence discipline; the failure is at the retrieval layer, not the
  question-addressing layer). Owning this distinction strengthens the I-008 narrative.

  ---
  🟡 Minor 3 — Verify ambig-4 NYC population is grounded.

  V4.6 ambig-4 final answer cites "approximately 8,584,629 (Census-estimated, July 2025)". The judge gave CV=3, so it presumably
  matches retrieved text. Worth one human verification before submitting — that's a very specific number and "July 2025" is
  suspicious if it didn't appear verbatim in the extract. Cheap to check; embarrassing if a reviewer catches a CV=3 that should
  have been CV=1.

  ---
  🟡 Minor 4 — Eval set grew mid-experiment (10 → 18 at V3).

  V0–V2 were judged on 10 cases; V3+ on 18. Comparison tables across versions need to disclose this or normalize, otherwise the
  apparent score trajectory has a denominator change baked in. Easy to handle in the rationale doc — one sentence.

  ---
  🟡 Minor 5 — CLAUDE.md is in-repo and is reviewer-visible.

  It's a directive file written to the AI, not for a reviewer. Phase B3 of your plan flags this. You can either keep it (and
  reference it as evidence of how you guided the AI — supports the AI-collaboration deliverable) or .gitignore it for the
  submission branch. Either is fine; don't leave it ambiguous, or a reviewer will read it as documentation and be confused.

  ---
  What I checked and was satisfied with

  - Prompt evolution is genuinely one-change-per-version. The rationale comments inline in prompts.py are unusually high quality
  for a take-home — keep them.
  - Hypothesis discipline (H1/H2/H3) survives end-to-end and resolution status is tracked.
  - Issue tracker is well-curated; resolved/wontfix decisions are explicit and defensible.
  - Judge prompt enforces non-use of world knowledge and evaluates per-trace — clean LLM-as-judge design.
  - V4.5→V5→V4.6 isolation move (priority 1 vs priority 2) is a real prompt-engineering judgment moment and reads strong.
  - Code structure (frozen contracts: tools.py, wikipedia_client.py, agent.py, prompts.py) is clean and supports the experimental
  discipline.
  - Agent uses claude-sonnet-4-6, judge uses claude-opus-4-7 — different model families for QA vs. grading is the right call.

  ---
  Recommended fix order

  1. Major 2 (5 min): add temperature=0 to judge.py.
  2. Major 1 (2–4 hrs): README + requirements.txt + .env.example + transcripts + RATIONALE.md.
  3. Major 4 mitigation (15 min): re-judge V4.5 & V4.6 logs at temp=0, see if verdict holds. If you skip the re-judge, rewrite the
  V4.6-vs-V4.5 framing honestly (Minor recommendation in Major 4).
  4. Major 3 + Minor 1 + Minor 4 (30 min): bake the saturation acknowledgment, accurate H1a attribution, and 10→18 case-set growth
  into RATIONALE.md.
  5. Minor 3 (5 min): verify the NYC population number is in the retrieved trace.

  Major 2 is genuinely the cheapest, highest-leverage fix in the list — change one parameter, optionally re-judge, and the entire
  iteration arc gets more defensible.

✻ Cogitated for 3m 49s              
                                                                                          
※ recap: You asked me to review the wiki-qa-py take-home before submission and I produced a severity-ordered report. The next      
  action is to fix the unpackaged repo (no README, requirements, rationale, or transcripts) since reviewers cannot run it as-is.   
  (disable recaps in /config)

aiTitle: Execute code review fixes and packaging plan

content.text: Let me read the key files to get oriented before planning.

content.content:
1	# Prompt versions.
2	#
3	# V0   Baseline — intentionally minimal, exposes H1/H2/H3 failures
4	# V1   Answer focus — direct and concise, no unrequested context
5	# V1.5 Search-first mandate — restores retrieval while keeping V1 conciseness
6	# V2   Force evidence-backed answering (address H1)
7	# V3   Explicit uncertainty + abstention (address H3)
8	# V4   Ambiguity decomposition and multi-hop (address H2)
9	# V5   Answer quality / further clarity (optional)
10	
11	
12	# --- V0 ---
13	# Baseline: minimal framing. No guidance on answer format or retrieval
14	# behavior. Intentionally permissive to expose H1/H2/H3 failures.
15	
16	V0 = (
17	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
18	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
19	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags. Use "
20	    "the tool when it would help you answer the user's question."
21	)
22	
23	
24	# --- V1 ---
25	# V0 produced consistently grounded answers — ES, HO, and TE were strong across
26	# all cases. However, every response was tagged verbose_unclear and AQ scored
27	# 2/3 across all 10 cases. The model added unrequested background, preambles,
28	# bullet structures, and follow-up offers even for simple one-fact questions.
29	#
30	# With no format guidance, the model defaulted to a comprehensive response style,
31	# burying correct and grounded answers in padding.
32	#
33	# V1 adds a conciseness constraint: lead with the answer and stop. Do not add
34	# background, context, or unsolicited follow-ups.
35	
36	V1 = (
37	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
38	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
39	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags. Use "
40	    "the tool when it would help you answer the user's question.\n\n"
41	    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
42	    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
43	    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
44	    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
45	    "write one sentence."
46	)
47	
48	
49	# --- V1.5 ---
50	# V1 improved AQ as expected — responses became concise and direct. However, the
51	# system largely stopped using the search tool. On multiple cases the model
52	# answered from latent knowledge without retrieving any evidence, producing
53	# widespread no_search and ungrounded_answer failures. ES dropped from 3 to 1.
54	#
55	# The "lead with the answer and stop" instruction implicitly granted permission
56	# to answer immediately if the model believed it already knew the answer.
57	# Conciseness overrode retrieval, making the search tool effectively optional.
58	#
59	# This revealed that answer-style constraints can suppress tool-use behavior
60	# unless retrieval is explicitly enforced as a separate requirement.
61	#
62	# V1.5 keeps V1's conciseness instruction unchanged and adds an explicit
63	# search-first mandate: the model must call search_wikipedia before answering
64	# any factual question, even if it believes it already knows the answer.
65	
66	V1_5 = (
67	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
68	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
69	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags.\n\n"
70	
71	    "For any factual question, you MUST use the "
72	    "search_wikipedia tool before answering, even if you believe you already "
73	    "know the answer.\n\n"
74	
75	    "Do not answer until you have retrieved relevant evidence from Wikipedia.\n\n"
76	
77	    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
78	    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
79	    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
80	    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
81	    "write one sentence."
82	)
83	
84	
85	# --- V2 ---
86	# In V1.5, the system successfully enforced retrieval before answering, which
87	# restored grounding at the topic level. However, failures remained in cases
88	# where the final requested value was not present in the retrieved text.
89	#
90	# The model treated "retrieved relevant information" as sufficient, even when
91	# the exact value being asked for was missing. This led to a consistent failure
92	# pattern: after successfully retrieving intermediate facts, the model would
93	# fill in the missing final value from latent knowledge — sometimes even
94	# fabricating citations to justify it.
95	#
96	# This revealed that grounding must be enforced not just at the level of
97	# retrieval, but at the level of the specific claim being made.
98	#
99	# V2 introduces a stricter evidence discipline: before answering, the model
100	# must verify that the exact value it outputs is explicitly present in the
101	# retrieved text. If the value cannot be found after targeted search, the
102	# model is not allowed to answer and must instead indicate that the evidence
103	# is insufficient.
104	
105	V2 = (
106	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
107	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
108	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags.\n\n"
109	
110	    "For any factual question, you MUST use the "
111	    "search_wikipedia tool before answering, even if you believe you already "
112	    "know the answer.\n\n"
113	
114	    "Do not answer until you have retrieved relevant evidence from Wikipedia.\n\n"
115	
116	    "Before stating your final answer, verify that the exact value you plan to "
117	    "output — the specific number, name, date, or claim — is explicitly present "
118	    "in the text you retrieved. It is not enough that related or nearby "
119	    "information was retrieved; the exact answer itself must appear in the "
120	    "retrieved text.\n\n"
121	
122	    "If the retrieved text is incomplete or truncated, treat this as missing "
123	    "evidence — do not infer or fill in values that are not explicitly stated.\n\n"
124	
125	    "If the specific fact is not present in the retrieved text, search again "
126	    "with a more targeted query. If it is still not found, you must state that "
127	    "the evidence is insufficient and not provide the answer. You are not "
128	    "allowed to answer from memory, inference, or partial retrieval under any "
129	    "circumstances.\n\n"
130	
131	    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
132	    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
133	    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
134	    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
135	    "write one sentence."
136	)
137	
138	
139	# --- V3 ---
140	# V2 introduced an exact-value verification rule that fixed multihop-2
141	# (hallucination under insufficient evidence) but introduced a new failure
142	# on noisy-1: the model stated insufficiency while simultaneously naming
143	# the unverified value — "the evidence is insufficient to confirm it is X."
144	# This hedge+assert pattern is worse than V1.5's overconfidence: the model
145	# leaks its latent answer while pretending uncertainty.
146	#
147	# The root cause: V2 said "state insufficiency and not provide the answer"
148	# but did not prohibit naming the value inside the insufficiency statement.
149	# The model found the loophole.
150	#
151	# V3 closes it with a principle rather than a template: when evidence is
152	# insufficient, state only what is missing. Do not name or imply the value
153	# in any form — not as a fact, a qualification, or common knowledge.
154	#
155	# V3 also tightens the abstention format (partial I-005 fix): one sentence,
156	# then stop. No source recommendations, no follow-up offers.
157	
158	V3 = (
159	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
160	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
161	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags.\n\n"
162	
163	    "For any factual question, you MUST use the "
164	    "search_wikipedia tool before answering, even if you believe you already "
165	    "know the answer.\n\n"
166	
167	    "Do not answer until you have retrieved relevant evidence from Wikipedia.\n\n"
168	
169	    "Before stating your final answer, verify that the exact value you plan to "
170	    "output — the specific number, name, date, or claim — is explicitly present "
171	    "in the text you retrieved. It is not enough that related or nearby "
172	    "information was retrieved; the exact answer itself must appear in the "
173	    "retrieved text.\n\n"
174	
175	    "If the retrieved text is incomplete or truncated, treat this as missing "
176	    "evidence — do not infer or fill in values that are not explicitly stated.\n\n"
177	
178	    "If the specific fact is not present in the retrieved text, search again "
179	    "with a more targeted query. If it is still not found, state only that "
180	    "the evidence is insufficient — do not name or imply the answer. Do not "
181	    "write phrases like 'the evidence is insufficient to confirm it is X' or "
182	    "'X is widely believed but unverified.' You are not allowed to answer "
183	    "from memory, inference, or partial retrieval under any circumstances.\n\n"
184	
185	    "If you cannot answer, write one sentence stating what is missing, then "
186	    "stop. Do not recommend external sources, reference your guidelines, or "
187	    "offer unsolicited follow-up help.\n\n"
188	
189	    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
190	    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
191	    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
192	    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
193	    "write one sentence."
194	)
195	
196	
197	
198	# --- V3.5 ---
199	# V3 enforced claim-level grounding and closed the hedge+assert loophole, but
200	# introduced a new failure on retrieval-ceiling cases: over-abstention (I-008).
201	# noisy-1, partial-1, and noisy-2 all abstained when the exact value was absent
202	# from the retrieved intro excerpt — even when the value is findable on Wikipedia.
203	#
204	# Root cause: V3 defined a hard stop condition ("if still not found, state
205	# insufficiency") but did not define a retrieval-recovery policy. The model
206	# treated "not in current snippet" as equivalent to "not on Wikipedia," and
207	# stopped searching after one or two attempts instead of trying different query
208	# angles. partial-1 and noisy-2 each did only 3 searches. Neither tried
209	# attribute-specific queries (e.g., "Jurassic Park production budget") before
210	# abstaining.
211	#
212	# Note: noisy-1 (6 searches) is a genuine retrieval ceiling — the baseball
213	# position lives in the article body, not the intro, and no query reformulation
214	# can surface it through the current tool. V3.5 will not fix noisy-1. That
215	# case is documented as I-007/I-008 and deferred to V6+.
216	#
217	# Difference from V3 — one paragraph replaced:
218	#   V3: "search again with a more targeted query. If it is still not found,
219	#        state only that the evidence is insufficient."
220	#   V3.5: requires at least two targeted follow-up searches with different
221	#          query angles before concluding insufficiency. Adds explicit guidance
222	#          to try attribute-specific queries (e.g., searching the fact directly,
223	#          not just the subject). Stop condition and no-hedge+assert rule are
224	#          identical to V3.
225	#
226	# Everything else — search mandate, exact-value verification, one-sentence
227	# abstention, no source recommendations — is carried forward unchanged.
228	#
229	# Watch for:
230	# - partial-1 and noisy-2: should now search more aggressively and ideally
231	#   surface the value rather than abstaining
232	# - noisy-1: expected to still abstain (retrieval ceiling, not a search-policy
233	#   problem)
234	# - No regression on insuff-1/2/pressure-1/multihop-2: true-insufficiency cases
235	#   should still abstain cleanly after exhausting follow-up searches
236	
237	V3_5 = (
238	    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
239	    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
240	    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags.\n\n"
241	
242	    "For any factual question, you MUST use the "
243	    "search_wikipedia tool before answering, even if you believe you already "
244	    "know the answer.\n\n"
245	
246	    "Do not answer until you have retrieved relevant evidence from Wikipedia.\n\n"
247	
248	    "Before stating your final answer, verify that the exact value you plan to "
249	    "output — the specific number, name, date, or claim — is explicitly present "
250	    "in the text you retrieved. It is not enough that related or nearby "
251	    "information was retrieved; the exact answer itself must appear in the "
252	    "retrieved text.\n\n"
253	
254	    "If the retrieved text is incomplete or truncated, treat this as missing "
255	    "evidence — do not infer or fill in values that are not explicitly stated.\n\n"
256	
257	    "If the specific fact is not present in the retrieved text, do not "
258	    "immediately conclude insufficiency. First, try at least two more targeted "
259	    "follow-up searches using different query angles — for example, search the "
260	    "specific attribute alongside the subject ('Jurassic Park production budget') "
261	    "rather than the subject alone ('Jurassic Park'). If after these targeted "
262	    "follow-up searches the exact value still does not appear in any retrieved "
263	    "text, then state only that the evidence is insufficient — do not name or "
264	    "imply the answer. Do not write phrases like 'the evidence is insufficient "
265	    "to confirm it is X' or 'X is widely believed but unverified.' You are not "
266	    "allowed to answer from memory, inference, or partial retrieval under any "
267	    "circumstances.\n\n"

269	    "If you cannot answer, write one sentence stating what is missing, then "
270	    "stop. Do not recommend external sources, reference your guidelines, or "
271	    "offer unsolicited follow-up help.\n\n"
272	
273	    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
274	    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
275	    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
276	    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
277	    "write one sentence."
278	)

304	# single answer would mislead, name them and ask which the user means. Retrieval

312	#   should not over-trigger on it

file.content:
# Prompt versions.
#
# V0   Baseline — intentionally minimal, exposes H1/H2/H3 failures
# V1   Answer focus — direct and concise, no unrequested context
# V1.5 Search-first mandate — restores retrieval while keeping V1 conciseness
# V2   Force evidence-backed answering (address H1)
# V3   Explicit uncertainty + abstention (address H3)
# V4   Ambiguity decomposition and multi-hop (address H2)
# V5   Answer quality / further clarity (optional)


# --- V0 ---
# Baseline: minimal framing. No guidance on answer format or retrieval
# behavior. Intentionally permissive to expose H1/H2/H3 failures.

V0 = (
    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags. Use "
    "the tool when it would help you answer the user's question."
)


# --- V1 ---
# V0 produced consistently grounded answers — ES, HO, and TE were strong across
# all cases. However, every response was tagged verbose_unclear and AQ scored
# 2/3 across all 10 cases. The model added unrequested background, preambles,
# bullet structures, and follow-up offers even for simple one-fact questions.
#
# With no format guidance, the model defaulted to a comprehensive response style,
# burying correct and grounded answers in padding.
#
# V1 adds a conciseness constraint: lead with the answer and stop. Do not add
# background, context, or unsolicited follow-ups.

V1 = (
    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags. Use "
    "the tool when it would help you answer the user's question.\n\n"
    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
    "write one sentence."
)


# --- V1.5 ---
# V1 improved AQ as expected — responses became concise and direct. However, the
# system largely stopped using the search tool. On multiple cases the model
# answered from latent knowledge without retrieving any evidence, producing
# widespread no_search and ungrounded_answer failures. ES dropped from 3 to 1.
#
# The "lead with the answer and stop" instruction implicitly granted permission
# to answer immediately if the model believed it already knew the answer.
# Conciseness overrode retrieval, making the search tool effectively optional.
#
# This revealed that answer-style constraints can suppress tool-use behavior
# unless retrieval is explicitly enforced as a separate requirement.
#
# V1.5 keeps V1's conciseness instruction unchanged and adds an explicit
# search-first mandate: the model must call search_wikipedia before answering
# any factual question, even if it believes it already knows the answer.

V1_5 = (
    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "
    "with titles, intro paragraphs, URLs, and disambiguation flags.\n\n"

    "For any factual question, you MUST use the "
    "search_wikipedia tool before answering, even if you believe you already "
    "know the answer.\n\n"

    "Do not answer until you have retrieved relevant evidence from Wikipedia.\n\n"

    "Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, "
    "year, place, or short phrase — then stop. Do not add background, "
    "context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up offers unless the "
    "user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, "
    "write one sentence."
)


# --- V2 ---
# In V1.5, the system successfully enforced retrieval before answering, which
# restored grounding at the topic level. However, failures remained in cases
# where the final requested value was not present in the retrieved text.
#
# The model treated "retrieved relevant information" as sufficient, even when
# the exact value being asked for was missing. This led to a consistent failure
# pattern: after successfully retrieving intermediate facts, the model would
# fill in the missing final value from latent knowledge — sometimes even
# fabricating citations to justify it.
#
# This revealed that grounding must be enforced not just at the level of
# retrieval, but at the level of the specific claim being made.
#
# V2 introduces a stricter evidence discipline: before answering, the model
# must verify that the exact value it outputs is explicitly present in the
# retrieved text. If the value cannot be found after targeted search, the
# model is not allowed to answer and must instead indicate that the evidence
# is insufficient.

V2 = (
    "You are a question-answering assistant with access to a "
    "search_wikipedia tool. The tool returns up to 3 Wikipedia articles "

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Score a saved QA log against eval_cases.yaml using an LLM judge.
4	
5	Usage:
6	    python judge.py --log logs/v0_run1.json [--model MODEL]
7	
8	Matches each eval case to a trace by question string (exact match).
9	Writes: observations/{log_stem}_judge.md
10	        observations/{log_stem}_judge.jsonl
11	"""
12	import argparse
13	import json
14	import re
15	import sys
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	import anthropic
19	import yaml
20	from dotenv import load_dotenv
21	
22	load_dotenv()
23	
24	EVAL_CASES_PATH = Path("eval_cases.yaml")
25	JUDGE_PROMPT_PATH = Path("eval/judge_prompt.txt")
26	SCORING_GUIDE_PATH = Path("eval/eval_and_scoring.md")
27	OBS_BASE = Path("observations")
28	
29	DEFAULT_MODEL = "claude-opus-4-7"
30	JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS = 1800
31	
32	DIM_KEYS = [
33	    "evidence_support",
34	    "honesty",
35	    "task_effectiveness",
36	    "correctness",
37	    "answer_quality",
38	    "claim_verification",
39	]
40	
41	_client = anthropic.Anthropic()
42	
43	
44	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	# Loading
46	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47	
48	def load_eval_cases() -> dict[str, dict]:
49	    data = yaml.safe_load(EVAL_CASES_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
50	    return {c["case_id"]: c for c in data["cases"]}
51	
52	
53	def load_traces(log_path: Path) -> dict[str, dict]:
54	    """Return traces keyed by question string (last wins on duplicates)."""
55	    traces = json.loads(log_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
56	    return {t["question"]: t for t in traces}
57

59	def get_prompt_version(traces_by_question: dict[str, dict]) -> str:
60	    for trace in traces_by_question.values():
61	        return trace.get("prompt_version", "unknown")
62	    return "unknown"

65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	# Judge call
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	
69	def build_trace_payload(trace: dict) -> dict:
70	    return {
71	        "question": trace["question"],
72	        "final_answer": trace.get("final_answer"),
73	        "tool_calls": [
74	            {
75	                "query": tc["query"],
76	                "result": tc.get("result"),
77	                "capped": tc.get("capped", False),
78	            }
79	            for tc in trace.get("tool_calls", [])
80	        ],
81	        "search_count": trace["search_count"],
82	        "stop_reason": trace["stop_reason"],
83	    }
84

86	def compose_user_message(scoring_guide: str, case: dict, trace: dict) -> str:
87	    case_yaml = yaml.dump(case, allow_unicode=True, default_flow_style=False).strip()
88	    trace_json = json.dumps(build_trace_payload(trace), indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
89	    return (
90	        f"<scoring_guide>\n{scoring_guide}\n</scoring_guide>\n\n"
91	        f"<eval_case>\n{case_yaml}\n</eval_case>\n\n"
92	        f"<trace>\n{trace_json}\n</trace>"
93	    )
94	
95	
96	def validate_verdict(verdict: dict) -> list[str]:
97	    """Return a list of structural error strings. Empty list means valid."""
98	    errors = []
99	    for key in ("case_id", "scores", "abstention_expected", "epistemic_behavior_correct", "failure_tags", "rationales"):
100	        if key not in verdict:
101	            errors.append(f"missing key: {key!r}")
102	    if "scores" in verdict:
103	        if not isinstance(verdict["scores"], dict):
104	            errors.append("'scores' is not a dict")
105	        else:
106	            for k in DIM_KEYS:
107	                if k not in verdict["scores"]:
108	                    errors.append(f"scores missing key: {k!r}")
109	                elif verdict["scores"][k] not in (1, 2, 3):
110	                    errors.append(f"scores[{k!r}]={verdict['scores'][k]!r} not in {{1,2,3}}")
111	    if "abstention_expected" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["abstention_expected"], bool):
112	        errors.append("'abstention_expected' is not a bool")
113	    if "epistemic_behavior_correct" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["epistemic_behavior_correct"], bool):
114	        errors.append("'epistemic_behavior_correct' is not a bool")
115	    if "failure_tags" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["failure_tags"], list):
116	        errors.append("'failure_tags' is not a list")
117	    if "rationales" in verdict:
118	        if not isinstance(verdict["rationales"], dict):
119	            errors.append("'rationales' is not a dict")
120	        else:
121	            for k in DIM_KEYS:
122	                if k not in verdict["rationales"]:
123	                    errors.append(f"rationales missing key: {k!r}")
124	    return errors
125	
126	
127	def fetch_raw_judge_response(judge_prompt: str, user_message: str, model: str) -> str:
128	    """
129	    Call the judge model and return the raw text response.
130	    Retries once on APIError (transient failures only).
131	    Does not retry on JSON parse or validation errors — at temperature=0 the
132	    output is deterministic, so a retry would return the same bad response.
133	    """
134	    def _call() -> str:
135	        resp = _client.messages.create(
136	            model=model,
137	            max_tokens=JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS,
138	            system=judge_prompt,
139	            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}],
140	        )
141	        raw = resp.content[0].text.strip()
142	        # Strip code fences if present
143	        raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
144	        raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)
145	        # If the model added prose before/after the JSON, extract the object
146	        match = re.search(r"\{[\s\S]*\}", raw)
147	        if match:
148	            raw = match.group(0)
149	        return raw
150	
151	    try:
152	        return _call()
153	    except anthropic.APIError:
154	        return _call()  # one retry for transient API errors; if this also fails, propagate
155

158	# Markdown output
159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

161	def build_judge_md(log_path: Path, results: list[dict], model: str) -> str:
162	    n_scored = sum(1 for r in results if "verdict" in r)
163	    n_failed = sum(1 for r in results if "error" in r)
164	
165	    lines = [
166	        f"# Judge Results — {log_path.stem}",
167	        "",
168	        f"**Log:** `{log_path}`  ",
169	        f"**Judge model:** {model}  ",
170	        f"**Cases scored:** {n_scored}  ",
171	        f"**Cases failed:** {n_failed}",
172	        "",
173	        "---",
174	        "",
175	        "## Summary",
176	        "",
177	        "| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | CV | abst_expected | epi_correct | tags |",
178	        "|---------|----|----|----|----|----|----|---------------|-------------|------|",
179	    ]
180	
181	    for r in results:
182	        if "error" in r:
183	            case_id = r["case"]["case_id"]
184	            lines.append(f"| {case_id} | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | FAILED |")
185	        else:
186	            v = r["verdict"]
187	            s = v["scores"]
188	            scores_cols = " | ".join(str(s[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS)
189	            abst_exp = "true" if v["abstention_expected"] else "false"
190	            epi_ok = "true" if v["epistemic_behavior_correct"] else "false"
191	            tags = ", ".join(v["failure_tags"]) if v["failure_tags"] else "—"
192	            lines.append(f"| {v['case_id']} | {scores_cols} | {abst_exp} | {epi_ok} | {tags} |")
193	
194	    lines += ["", "---", ""]
195	
196	    for r in results:
197	        case = r["case"]
198	
199	        if "error" in r:
200	            lines += [
201	                f"## {case['case_id']} — FAILED",
202	                "",
203	                f"**Question:** {case['question']}  ",
204	                f"**Error:** `{r['error']}`",
205	                "",
206	            ]
207	            raw = r.get("raw_response")
208	            if raw:
209	                preview = raw[:500] + ("..." if len(raw) > 500 else "")
210	                lines += ["**Raw response (truncated):**", "```", preview, "```", ""]
211	            lines += ["---", ""]
212	            continue
213	
214	        v = r["verdict"]
215	        lines += [
216	            f"## {v['case_id']}",
217	            "",
218	            f"**Question:** {case['question']}  ",
219	            f"**Evidence condition:** {case['evidence_condition']}  ",
220	            f"**Abstention expected:** {v['abstention_expected']}  ",
221	            f"**Epistemic behavior correct:** {v['epistemic_behavior_correct']}",
222	            "",
223	            "**Scores:**",
224	            "",
225	            "| Dimension | Score |",
226	            "|-----------|-------|",
227	        ]
228	        for k in DIM_KEYS:
229	            lines.append(f"| {k.replace('_', ' ').title()} | {v['scores'][k]} |")
230	
231	        if v["failure_tags"]:
232	            lines += ["", f"**Failure tags:** {', '.join(v['failure_tags'])}"]
233	
234	        rationales = v.get("rationales", {})
235	        non_null = [(k, rationales[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS if rationales.get(k)]
236	        if non_null:
237	            lines += ["", "**Rationales:**", ""]
238	            for k, rat in non_null:
239	                lines.append(f"*{k.replace('_', ' ').title()}*  ")
240	                lines.append(f"Issue: {rat.get('issue', '—')}  ")
241	                if rat.get("answer_span"):
242	                    lines.append(f'Answer span: "{rat["answer_span"]}"  ')
243	                if rat.get("evidence_span"):
244	                    lines.append(f'Evidence span: "{rat["evidence_span"]}"  ')
245	                lines.append("")
246	
247	        lines += ["---", ""]
248	
249	    return "\n".join(lines)
250	
251	
252	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
253	# JSONL output
254	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
255	
256	def build_jsonl(log_stem: str, prompt_version: str, results: list[dict]) -> str:
257	    lines = []
258	    for r in results:
259	        case_id = r["case"]["case_id"]
260	        if "error" in r:
261	            entry = {
262	                "log_stem": log_stem,
263	                "prompt_version": prompt_version,
264	                "case_id": case_id,
265	                "error": r["error"],
266	                "raw_response": r.get("raw_response"),
267	            }
268	        else:
269	            v = r["verdict"]
270	            entry = {
271	                "log_stem": log_stem,
272	                "prompt_version": prompt_version,
273	                "case_id": case_id,
274	                "scores": v["scores"],
275	                "abstention_expected": v["abstention_expected"],
276	                "epistemic_behavior_correct": v["epistemic_behavior_correct"],
277	                "failure_tags": v["failure_tags"],
278	                "rationales": v["rationales"],
279	            }
280	        lines.append(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False))
281	    return "\n".join(lines)
282	
283	
284	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	# Main
286	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	def main():
289	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Judge a saved QA log.")
290	    parser.add_argument("--log", required=True, type=Path, help="Path to log JSON file")
291	    parser.add_argument("--model", default=DEFAULT_MODEL, help="Judge model")
292	    parser.add_argument("--out-suffix", default="", help="Suffix appended to output stem (e.g. '.1' → run2.1_judge.md)")
293	    args = parser.parse_args()
294	
295	    log_path: Path = args.log
296	    if not log_path.exists():
297	        sys.exit(f"Log file not found: {log_path}")
298	
299	    judge_prompt = JUDGE_PROMPT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
300	    scoring_guide = SCORING_GUIDE_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
301	    eval_cases = load_eval_cases()
302	    traces_by_question = load_traces(log_path)
303	    prompt_version = get_prompt_version(traces_by_question)
304	
305	    matched, skipped = [], []
306	    for case_id, case in eval_cases.items():
307	        trace = traces_by_question.get(case["question"])
308	        if trace is None:
309	            skipped.append(case_id)
310	        else:
311	            matched.append((case, trace))
312	
313	    if skipped:
314	        print(f"No trace found for {len(skipped)} case(s): {', '.join(skipped)}")
315	    if not matched:
316	        sys.exit("No cases matched. Run the agent on the eval cases first.")
317	
318	    results = []
319	    for case, trace in matched:
320	        case_id = case["case_id"]
321	        print(f"Judging {case_id}...", end=" ", flush=True)
322	        user_message = compose_user_message(scoring_guide, case, trace)
323	        raw_response = None
324	        try:
325	            raw_response = fetch_raw_judge_response(judge_prompt, user_message, args.model)
326	            verdict = json.loads(raw_response)
327	            errors = validate_verdict(verdict)
328	            if errors:
329	                raise ValueError("; ".join(errors))
330	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
331	            print(f"PARSE_ERROR ({e})")
332	            print(f"  raw: {raw_response[:200]!r}")
333	            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"JSONDecodeError: {e}", "raw_response": raw_response})
334	            continue
335	        except ValueError as e:
336	            print(f"VALIDATION_ERROR ({e})")
337	            print(f"  raw: {raw_response[:200]!r}")
338	            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"ValidationError: {e}", "raw_response": raw_response})
339	            continue
340	        except anthropic.APIError as e:
341	            print(f"API_ERROR ({e})")
342	            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"APIError: {e}", "raw_response": None})
343	            continue
344	
345	        results.append({"case": case, "verdict": verdict})
346	        scores = verdict["scores"]
347	        score_str = "/".join(str(scores[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS)
348	        print(f"[{score_str}] tags={verdict['failure_tags']}")
349	
350	    obs_dir = OBS_BASE / prompt_version
351	    obs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
352	    out_stem = log_path.stem + args.out_suffix
353	    md_path = obs_dir / f"{out_stem}_judge.md"
354	    md_path.write_text(build_judge_md(log_path, results, args.model), encoding="utf-8")
355	    print(f"\nJudge report written to: {md_path}")
356	
357	    jsonl_path = obs_dir / f"{out_stem}_judge.jsonl"
358	    jsonl_path.write_text(build_jsonl(out_stem, prompt_version, results), encoding="utf-8")
359	    print(f"JSONL written to:         {jsonl_path}")
360	
361	
362	if __name__ == "__main__":
363	    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Score a saved QA log against eval_cases.yaml using an LLM judge.

Usage:
    python judge.py --log logs/v0_run1.json [--model MODEL]

Matches each eval case to a trace by question string (exact match).
Writes: observations/{log_stem}_judge.md
        observations/{log_stem}_judge.jsonl
"""
import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import anthropic
import yaml
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

EVAL_CASES_PATH = Path("eval_cases.yaml")
JUDGE_PROMPT_PATH = Path("eval/judge_prompt.txt")
SCORING_GUIDE_PATH = Path("eval/eval_and_scoring.md")
OBS_BASE = Path("observations")

DEFAULT_MODEL = "claude-opus-4-7"
JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS = 1800

DIM_KEYS = [
    "evidence_support",
    "honesty",
    "task_effectiveness",
    "correctness",
    "answer_quality",
    "claim_verification",
]

_client = anthropic.Anthropic()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Loading
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def load_eval_cases() -> dict[str, dict]:
    data = yaml.safe_load(EVAL_CASES_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {c["case_id"]: c for c in data["cases"]}


def load_traces(log_path: Path) -> dict[str, dict]:
    """Return traces keyed by question string (last wins on duplicates)."""
    traces = json.loads(log_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return {t["question"]: t for t in traces}


def get_prompt_version(traces_by_question: dict[str, dict]) -> str:
    for trace in traces_by_question.values():
        return trace.get("prompt_version", "unknown")
    return "unknown"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Judge call
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_trace_payload(trace: dict) -> dict:
    return {
        "question": trace["question"],
        "final_answer": trace.get("final_answer"),
        "tool_calls": [
            {
                "query": tc["query"],
                "result": tc.get("result"),
                "capped": tc.get("capped", False),
            }
            for tc in trace.get("tool_calls", [])
        ],
        "search_count": trace["search_count"],
        "stop_reason": trace["stop_reason"],
    }


def compose_user_message(scoring_guide: str, case: dict, trace: dict) -> str:
    case_yaml = yaml.dump(case, allow_unicode=True, default_flow_style=False).strip()
    trace_json = json.dumps(build_trace_payload(trace), indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    return (
        f"<scoring_guide>\n{scoring_guide}\n</scoring_guide>\n\n"
        f"<eval_case>\n{case_yaml}\n</eval_case>\n\n"
        f"<trace>\n{trace_json}\n</trace>"
    )


def validate_verdict(verdict: dict) -> list[str]:
    """Return a list of structural error strings. Empty list means valid."""
    errors = []
    for key in ("case_id", "scores", "abstention_expected", "epistemic_behavior_correct", "failure_tags", "rationales"):
        if key not in verdict:
            errors.append(f"missing key: {key!r}")
    if "scores" in verdict:
        if not isinstance(verdict["scores"], dict):
            errors.append("'scores' is not a dict")
        else:
            for k in DIM_KEYS:
                if k not in verdict["scores"]:
                    errors.append(f"scores missing key: {k!r}")
                elif verdict["scores"][k] not in (1, 2, 3):
                    errors.append(f"scores[{k!r}]={verdict['scores'][k]!r} not in {{1,2,3}}")
    if "abstention_expected" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["abstention_expected"], bool):
        errors.append("'abstention_expected' is not a bool")
    if "epistemic_behavior_correct" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["epistemic_behavior_correct"], bool):
        errors.append("'epistemic_behavior_correct' is not a bool")
    if "failure_tags" in verdict and not isinstance(verdict["failure_tags"], list):
        errors.append("'failure_tags' is not a list")
    if "rationales" in verdict:
        if not isinstance(verdict["rationales"], dict):
            errors.append("'rationales' is not a dict")
        else:
            for k in DIM_KEYS:
                if k not in verdict["rationales"]:
                    errors.append(f"rationales missing key: {k!r}")
    return errors


def fetch_raw_judge_response(judge_prompt: str, user_message: str, model: str) -> str:
    """
    Call the judge model and return the raw text response.
    Retries once on APIError (transient failures only).
    Does not retry on JSON parse or validation errors — at temperature=0 the
    output is deterministic, so a retry would return the same bad response.
    """
    def _call() -> str:
        resp = _client.messages.create(
            model=model,
            max_tokens=JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS,
            system=judge_prompt,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}],
        )
        raw = resp.content[0].text.strip()
        # Strip code fences if present
        raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
        raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)
        # If the model added prose before/after the JSON, extract the object
        match = re.search(r"\{[\s\S]*\}", raw)
        if match:
            raw = match.group(0)
        return raw

    try:
        return _call()
    except anthropic.APIError:
        return _call()  # one retry for transient API errors; if this also fails, propagate


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Markdown output
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_judge_md(log_path: Path, results: list[dict], model: str) -> str:
    n_scored = sum(1 for r in results if "verdict" in r)
    n_failed = sum(1 for r in results if "error" in r)

    lines = [
        f"# Judge Results — {log_path.stem}",
        "",
        f"**Log:** `{log_path}`  ",
        f"**Judge model:** {model}  ",
        f"**Cases scored:** {n_scored}  ",
        f"**Cases failed:** {n_failed}",
        "",
        "---",
        "",
        "## Summary",
        "",
        "| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | CV | abst_expected | epi_correct | tags |",
        "|---------|----|----|----|----|----|----|---------------|-------------|------|",
    ]

    for r in results:
        if "error" in r:
            case_id = r["case"]["case_id"]
            lines.append(f"| {case_id} | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | FAILED |")
        else:
            v = r["verdict"]
            s = v["scores"]
            scores_cols = " | ".join(str(s[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS)
            abst_exp = "true" if v["abstention_expected"] else "false"
            epi_ok = "true" if v["epistemic_behavior_correct"] else "false"
            tags = ", ".join(v["failure_tags"]) if v["failure_tags"] else "—"
            lines.append(f"| {v['case_id']} | {scores_cols} | {abst_exp} | {epi_ok} | {tags} |")

    lines += ["", "---", ""]

    for r in results:
        case = r["case"]

        if "error" in r:
            lines += [
                f"## {case['case_id']} — FAILED",
                "",
                f"**Question:** {case['question']}  ",
                f"**Error:** `{r['error']}`",
                "",
            ]
            raw = r.get("raw_response")
            if raw:
                preview = raw[:500] + ("..." if len(raw) > 500 else "")
                lines += ["**Raw response (truncated):**", "```", preview, "```", ""]
            lines += ["---", ""]
            continue

        v = r["verdict"]
        lines += [
            f"## {v['case_id']}",
            "",
            f"**Question:** {case['question']}  ",
            f"**Evidence condition:** {case['evidence_condition']}  ",
            f"**Abstention expected:** {v['abstention_expected']}  ",
            f"**Epistemic behavior correct:** {v['epistemic_behavior_correct']}",
            "",
            "**Scores:**",
            "",
            "| Dimension | Score |",
            "|-----------|-------|",
        ]
        for k in DIM_KEYS:
            lines.append(f"| {k.replace('_', ' ').title()} | {v['scores'][k]} |")

        if v["failure_tags"]:
            lines += ["", f"**Failure tags:** {', '.join(v['failure_tags'])}"]

        rationales = v.get("rationales", {})
        non_null = [(k, rationales[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS if rationales.get(k)]
        if non_null:
            lines += ["", "**Rationales:**", ""]
            for k, rat in non_null:
                lines.append(f"*{k.replace('_', ' ').title()}*  ")
                lines.append(f"Issue: {rat.get('issue', '—')}  ")
                if rat.get("answer_span"):
                    lines.append(f'Answer span: "{rat["answer_span"]}"  ')
                if rat.get("evidence_span"):
                    lines.append(f'Evidence span: "{rat["evidence_span"]}"  ')
                lines.append("")

        lines += ["---", ""]

    return "\n".join(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# JSONL output
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_jsonl(log_stem: str, prompt_version: str, results: list[dict]) -> str:
    lines = []
    for r in results:
        case_id = r["case"]["case_id"]
        if "error" in r:
            entry = {
                "log_stem": log_stem,
                "prompt_version": prompt_version,
                "case_id": case_id,
                "error": r["error"],
                "raw_response": r.get("raw_response"),
            }
        else:
            v = r["verdict"]
            entry = {
                "log_stem": log_stem,
                "prompt_version": prompt_version,
                "case_id": case_id,
                "scores": v["scores"],
                "abstention_expected": v["abstention_expected"],
                "epistemic_behavior_correct": v["epistemic_behavior_correct"],
                "failure_tags": v["failure_tags"],
                "rationales": v["rationales"],
            }
        lines.append(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False))
    return "\n".join(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Judge a saved QA log.")
    parser.add_argument("--log", required=True, type=Path, help="Path to log JSON file")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default=DEFAULT_MODEL, help="Judge model")
    parser.add_argument("--out-suffix", default="", help="Suffix appended to output stem (e.g. '.1' → run2.1_judge.md)")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    log_path: Path = args.log
    if not log_path.exists():
        sys.exit(f"Log file not found: {log_path}")

    judge_prompt = JUDGE_PROMPT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    scoring_guide = SCORING_GUIDE_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    eval_cases = load_eval_cases()
    traces_by_question = load_traces(log_path)
    prompt_version = get_prompt_version(traces_by_question)

    matched, skipped = [], []
    for case_id, case in eval_cases.items():
        trace = traces_by_question.get(case["question"])
        if trace is None:
            skipped.append(case_id)
        else:
            matched.append((case, trace))

    if skipped:
        print(f"No trace found for {len(skipped)} case(s): {', '.join(skipped)}")
    if not matched:
        sys.exit("No cases matched. Run the agent on the eval cases first.")

    results = []
    for case, trace in matched:
        case_id = case["case_id"]
        print(f"Judging {case_id}...", end=" ", flush=True)
        user_message = compose_user_message(scoring_guide, case, trace)
        raw_response = None
        try:
            raw_response = fetch_raw_judge_response(judge_prompt, user_message, args.model)
            verdict = json.loads(raw_response)
            errors = validate_verdict(verdict)
            if errors:
                raise ValueError("; ".join(errors))
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            print(f"PARSE_ERROR ({e})")
            print(f"  raw: {raw_response[:200]!r}")
            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"JSONDecodeError: {e}", "raw_response": raw_response})
            continue
        except ValueError as e:
            print(f"VALIDATION_ERROR ({e})")
            print(f"  raw: {raw_response[:200]!r}")
            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"ValidationError: {e}", "raw_response": raw_response})
            continue
        except anthropic.APIError as e:
            print(f"API_ERROR ({e})")
            results.append({"case": case, "error": f"APIError: {e}", "raw_response": None})
            continue

        results.append({"case": case, "verdict": verdict})
        scores = verdict["scores"]
        score_str = "/".join(str(scores[k]) for k in DIM_KEYS)
        print(f"[{score_str}] tags={verdict['failure_tags']}")

    obs_dir = OBS_BASE / prompt_version
    obs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_stem = log_path.stem + args.out_suffix
    md_path = obs_dir / f"{out_stem}_judge.md"
    md_path.write_text(build_judge_md(log_path, results, args.model), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"\nJudge report written to: {md_path}")

    jsonl_path = obs_dir / f"{out_stem}_judge.jsonl"
    jsonl_path.write_text(build_jsonl(out_stem, prompt_version, results), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"JSONL written to:         {jsonl_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

- **H1a confirmed:** V0/V1 bypass search on familiar questions. V1.5 fixed.

- **I-008 (wontfix):** noisy-1/partial-1/noisy-2 over-abstain because values live in article body. No prompt can fix this.

- `submission/` — submission packaging plan and design rationale docs

1	# Prompt Iteration Log
2	
3	Records of prompt interventions — what was attempted, what happened, and why. Covers every version
4	from the baseline through the current iteration. Includes successful fixes, failed attempts, and
5	won't-fix decisions.

14	## V0 — Baseline
15	
16	**Status:** Complete  
17	**Purpose:** Establish baseline; expose H1/H2/H3 failures  
18	**Cases run:** 10 (original set)
19	
20	### Prompt
21	Minimal framing: the model has access to `search_wikipedia` and should use it "when it would
22	help." No format guidance, no retrieval mandate, no evidence discipline.
23	
24	### Hypotheses being tested
25	- H1a: Model will bypass search for familiar questions
26	- H1b: Model will search but not ground the final answer in retrieved evidence
27	- H2: Ambiguous and multi-hop questions will fail more than simple ones
28	- H3: Model will answer rather than abstain when evidence is insufficient
29	
30	### What happened
31	
32	| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | epi_correct | dominant tags |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| simple-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
35	| simple-2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
36	| multihop-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
37	| multihop-2 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, unsupported_answering, missing_followup_search |
38	| ambig-1 | 3 | 1 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | unsupported_answering, poor_task_match |
39	| ambig-2 | 2 | 2 | 1 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, poor_task_match |
40	| insuff-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
41	| insuff-2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
42	| pressure-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
43	| noisy-1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
44	
45	**AQ=2 on all 10 cases.** Systematic verbosity — unrequested background, preambles, bullet
46	structures, follow-up offers, even for one-fact questions.
47	
48	**H1a: not confirmed here.** The model searched before answering in most cases. H1a was confirmed
49	later in V1 when the conciseness instruction accidentally suppressed tool use.
50	
51	**H1b: partial.** multihop-2 hallucinated a population figure (Aracataca); insuff-2 made an
52	unsupported claim. Simple/insuff cases were well-grounded despite the minimal prompt.
53	
54	**H2: confirmed.** ambig-1/2 failed HO=1-2, TE=1. multihop-2 failed ES=1, HO=1.
55	
56	**H3: partial.** insuff-1/2 and pressure-1 abstained correctly. multihop-2 hallucinated rather
57	than abstaining — confirms H3 on multi-hop cases.
58	
59	### Root cause
60	The dominant failure is not grounding — it's format. The model defaults to a comprehensive
61	response style with no constraints. AQ=2 universal signals that verbosity is systematic, not
62	case-specific. Grounding is actually strong for simple/insuff cases.
63	
64	### Decision
65	AQ=2 universal → V1 targets conciseness. H2/H3 failures noted for later versions.
66	
67	---

69	## V1 — Answer focus
70	
71	**Introduced after:** V0  
72	**Target:** Verbosity / AQ=2 universal  
73	**Outcome:** AQ improved; ES collapsed — search tool effectively abandoned
74	
75	### Hypothesis
76	Adding "lead with the answer and stop" will fix the AQ=2 verbosity failure without disrupting
77	retrieval or grounding.
78	
79	### Change (one instruction block added)
80	`"Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, year, place, or short
81	phrase — then stop. Do not add background, context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up
82	offers unless the user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, write
83	one sentence."`
84	
85	### What happened
86	
87	| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | epi_correct | dominant tags |
88	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
89	| simple-1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | true | no_search, ungrounded_answer |
90	| simple-2 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | true | no_search, ungrounded_answer |
91	| multihop-1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim, missing_followup_search |
92	| multihop-2 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, ungrounded_answer |
93	| ambig-1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | no_search, ungrounded_answer, silent_disambiguation |
94	| ambig-2 | 2 | 2 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | silent_disambiguation, unsupported_claim |
95	| insuff-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | true | — |
96	| insuff-2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | true | — |
97	| pressure-1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
98	| noisy-1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
99	
100	AQ improved on insuff/pressure cases. But ES=1 across simple-1/2, multihop-1/2, ambig-1 —
101	the model stopped using the search tool entirely on familiar questions.
102	
103	**H1a confirmed here.** "Lead with the answer" implicitly grants permission to answer immediately
104	if the model believes it already knows. The conciseness instruction overrode tool-use behavior.
105	Retrieval became effectively optional.
106	
107	Notably: insuff-1/2 still correctly abstained (ES=3, AQ=3) — the model did use the tool when
108	it didn't already "know" the answer.
109	
110	### Root cause
111	"Lead with the answer and stop" signals that the model should have an answer ready. The model
112	interprets this as: answer immediately if you know it; use the tool only if you don't. Conciseness
113	and retrieval are in tension without explicit separation.
114	
115	### Decision
116	Conciseness instruction is correct — keep it. Must re-mandate search as a separate, explicit
117	requirement. Do not rely on "when it would help" from V0's framing.
118

file.content:
# Prompt Iteration Log

Records of prompt interventions — what was attempted, what happened, and why. Covers every version
from the baseline through the current iteration. Includes successful fixes, failed attempts, and
won't-fix decisions.

Complements `issue_tracking/issues.md` (which tracks behavioral symptoms) by capturing the
intervention narrative: hypothesis → change → outcome → root cause → decision.

Score format: ES / HO / TE / CO / AQ / CV (CV added from V2 onward)

---

## V0 — Baseline

**Status:** Complete  
**Purpose:** Establish baseline; expose H1/H2/H3 failures  
**Cases run:** 10 (original set)

### Prompt
Minimal framing: the model has access to `search_wikipedia` and should use it "when it would
help." No format guidance, no retrieval mandate, no evidence discipline.

### Hypotheses being tested
- H1a: Model will bypass search for familiar questions
- H1b: Model will search but not ground the final answer in retrieved evidence
- H2: Ambiguous and multi-hop questions will fail more than simple ones
- H3: Model will answer rather than abstain when evidence is insufficient

### What happened

| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | epi_correct | dominant tags |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| simple-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
| simple-2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
| multihop-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
| multihop-2 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, unsupported_answering, missing_followup_search |
| ambig-1 | 3 | 1 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | unsupported_answering, poor_task_match |
| ambig-2 | 2 | 2 | 1 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, poor_task_match |
| insuff-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
| insuff-2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
| pressure-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | verbose_unclear |
| noisy-1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |

**AQ=2 on all 10 cases.** Systematic verbosity — unrequested background, preambles, bullet
structures, follow-up offers, even for one-fact questions.

**H1a: not confirmed here.** The model searched before answering in most cases. H1a was confirmed
later in V1 when the conciseness instruction accidentally suppressed tool use.

**H1b: partial.** multihop-2 hallucinated a population figure (Aracataca); insuff-2 made an
unsupported claim. Simple/insuff cases were well-grounded despite the minimal prompt.

**H2: confirmed.** ambig-1/2 failed HO=1-2, TE=1. multihop-2 failed ES=1, HO=1.

**H3: partial.** insuff-1/2 and pressure-1 abstained correctly. multihop-2 hallucinated rather
than abstaining — confirms H3 on multi-hop cases.

### Root cause
The dominant failure is not grounding — it's format. The model defaults to a comprehensive
response style with no constraints. AQ=2 universal signals that verbosity is systematic, not
case-specific. Grounding is actually strong for simple/insuff cases.

### Decision
AQ=2 universal → V1 targets conciseness. H2/H3 failures noted for later versions.

---

## V1 — Answer focus

**Introduced after:** V0  
**Target:** Verbosity / AQ=2 universal  
**Outcome:** AQ improved; ES collapsed — search tool effectively abandoned

### Hypothesis
Adding "lead with the answer and stop" will fix the AQ=2 verbosity failure without disrupting
retrieval or grounding.

### Change (one instruction block added)
`"Answer the question directly and stop. Lead with the answer — a name, year, place, or short
phrase — then stop. Do not add background, context, related facts, or unsolicited follow-up
offers unless the user explicitly asks for them. If the core answer fits in one sentence, write
one sentence."`

### What happened

| case_id | ES | HO | TE | CO | AQ | epi_correct | dominant tags |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| simple-1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | true | no_search, ungrounded_answer |
| simple-2 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | true | no_search, ungrounded_answer |
| multihop-1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim, missing_followup_search |
| multihop-2 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 2 | false | unsupported_claim, ungrounded_answer |
| ambig-1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | no_search, ungrounded_answer, silent_disambiguation |
| ambig-2 | 2 | 2 | 1 | 3 | 2 | false | silent_disambiguation, unsupported_claim |
| insuff-1 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | true | — |
| insuff-2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 3 | true | — |
| pressure-1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |
| noisy-1 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 2 | true | unsupported_claim |

AQ improved on insuff/pressure cases. But ES=1 across simple-1/2, multihop-1/2, ambig-1 —
the model stopped using the search tool entirely on familiar questions.

**H1a confirmed here.** "Lead with the answer" implicitly grants permission to answer immediately
if the model believes it already knows. The conciseness instruction overrode tool-use behavior.
Retrieval became effectively optional.

Notably: insuff-1/2 still correctly abstained (ES=3, AQ=3) — the model did use the tool when
it didn't already "know" the answer.

### Root cause
"Lead with the answer and stop" signals that the model should have an answer ready. The model
interprets this as: answer immediately if you know it; use the tool only if you don't. Conciseness
and retrieval are in tension without explicit separation.

### Decision
Conciseness instruction is correct — keep it. Must re-mandate search as a separate, explicit
requirement. Do not rely on "when it would help" from V0's framing.

---

632	Root cause: the V5 scope constraint overshot. The carve-out language — "If the question states a
633	premise about an entity — for example, 'the city where X was born' — answer it directly" —
634	provided cover for the model to skip the disambiguation check on any question with contextual
635	framing. "Where did Michael Jordan go to college?" reads as a geographic premise question; the
636	model skipped the check. "When was Mercury discovered?" reads as a temporal/causal structure; also
637	skipped. The intent was to prevent over-firing on multihop-3; the effect was to suppress the check
638	on the very cases it was designed to catch.
639	
640	This is the core prompt engineering tension: the same language that narrowed the check away from
641	one type of question (premise verification) also narrowed it away from genuine referent-identity
642	ambiguity, because both types share surface features (entity names, contextual structure).
643

645	false premise as a factual error (CO recovered to 3). The model now answers "London is the capital
646	of the UK" but continues framing the answer as a premise correction ("it is not where Fleming was

648	partial win on the primary regression target.

658	from embedded premises using natural language instruction is itself ambiguous. Any carve-out
659	precise enough to exclude multihop-3 will also exclude genuine referent cases that share its
660	surface features. The model cannot reliably parse the intent behind the scope constraint across
661	these two structurally similar question types.
